title: The Voice of Reality
learningGoal: The learner will master pragmatic intonation shifting, differentiating between the high-pitch rising contours of polite transactional requests and the low-pitch, hard-stressed imperatives required during emergencies.
instructions: In this final pronunciation class, we are not focusing on single letters. We are focusing on your 'Tune' (Intonation). How you say something changes its meaning! Make your voice go UP to be polite. Make your voice loud and heavy to report an emergency.

contentItems.text: If you ask for help with a flat, bored voice, people will think you are angry. You must smile with your voice!

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: माफ़ करें? (ध्यान आकर्षित करने के लिए)
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The pitch MUST rise on the word 'Me'. Ex-cuse MEE^?
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying it flatly, which sounds like an insult.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या आप मेरी मदद कर सकते हैं?
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Gentle, polite tone. Rise the pitch slightly at the end.
Smooth, friendly flow. 'I would' sounds like 'I'd'. I'd like the CHIC-ken.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying it like an aggressive robot order.

targetPhrasesOrWords.targetText: Can I have the bill, please?
targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: कैन आई हैव द बिल, प्लीज़?
targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: क्या मुझे बिल मिल सकता है?
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The word 'Please' should have a happy, rising tone. PleeEEzz^.
targetPhrasesOrWords.syllableSplit: can i HAVE the BILL, pleeEEz^

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Pitch MUST rise at the end because it's a Yes/No question! Cards^?
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Dropping the pitch at the end, turning it into a statement.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: NO RISING PITCH. This is an emergency. Loud, hard stress on CALL and AM. 'CALL an AM-bu-lance!'
Serious tone. Stress NEED and DOC.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: मेरा बैग गायब (चोरी) हो गया है।

targetPhrasesOrWords.targetText: I am looking for the train.
targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: आई एम लुकिंग फॉर द ट्रेन।
targetPhrasesOrWords.syllableSplit: I am LOOK-ing for the TRAIN

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Warm, descending tone. Smile while saying it.

expectedPronunciationText: Excuse me Could you help me I would like the chicken Call the police

feedbackRules.onPass: Awesome! You mastered the 'tune' of English! You sounded incredibly polite when asking for things, and powerfully urgent when shouting for help during the emergencies.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! You must change the pitch of your voice. For Yes/No questions ('Do you take cards?'), your voice MUST go UP at the end! For emergencies ('Call the police!'), it must stay flat, loud, and serious.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for 'Excuse me?'. If you want to get someone's attention, the 'Me' should have a high, singing pitch! Otherwise, they might think you are angry.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: excuse me could you help me where is the bathroom i would like the chicken can i have the bill please how much is this do you take cards call an ambulance call the police i need a doctor help me my bag is gone i am looking for the train i am lost thank you so much
acceptedVariants: excuse me could you help me where is the bathroom i would like the chicken can i have the bill please how much is this do you take cards call an ambulance call the police i need a doctor help me my bag is gone i am looking for the train i am lost thank you so much
adminCorrectAnswerSet.phoneticApproximation: Evaluates pragmatic intonation contours—specifically testing the terminal pitch rise on polite modals (Could you help me^?) versus the flat, high-amplitude stress of imperatives (CALL the po-LICE!).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Excuse me? Could you help me...

hintText: Smile while practicing the Restaurant phrases! Have a serious face when practicing the Emergency phrases!
commonMistakes: Using flat declarative intonation on polite interrogatives, resulting in an aggressive sonic footprint.
